title: Foreign aid given per capita vs. GDP per capita
subtitle: [Net](#dod:net-oda) [official development assistance (ODA)](#dod:oda), expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.
caption:
Foreign aid given per capita vs. GDP per capita

Net official development assistance (ODA), expressed in US dollars and adjusted for inflation.

This scatter plot maps countries based on their GDP per capita on the horizontal axis and net official development assistance given per capita on the vertical axis. The horizontal axis ranges from $0 to $120,000 in 2021 international dollars, while the vertical axis spans from $0 to over $800 in constant 2023 US dollars. Data points are color-coded by region, with Europe represented in blue, Asia in teal, North America in orange, and Oceania in light blue. Circle sizes correspond to population, with a reference scale indicating a 300 million population size. Norway appears at the highest position on the vertical axis, exceeding $800 in aid per capita, while positioned near $90,000 on the horizontal axis. Luxembourg and Ireland are located at the far right, with GDP per capita values approaching or exceeding $110,000 and aid per capita between $400 and $500. Switzerland and Sweden cluster in the upper middle section, with GDP per capita between $60,000 and $80,000 and aid per capita ranging from $400 to $500. The United States is represented by a large orange circle near $80,000 GDP per capita and approximately $200 aid per capita. Asian countries such as Japan, South Korea, and Saudi Arabia group between $40,000 and $70,000 GDP per capita with aid per capita generally below $200. Thailand and Kazakhstan appear at the lower left, with GDP per capita under $40,000 and minimal aid per capita. Qatar is positioned near $110,000 GDP per capita with aid per capita around $250.
